subject: DevOps
title: MySpace Loses 12 Years of Music
description: A 60-second lesson on MySpace Loses 12 Years of Music.

hook.text: In 2019, MySpace admitted 50 million songs from 2003-2015 were lost in a server migration.

buildup.text: During the migration, audio files were corrupted or deleted. No one noticed right away.

discovery.text: Users found their music missing. MySpace blamed a migration issue. The data was gone forever.

twist.text: Early recordings of now-famous artists and rare demos were lost. Irreplaceable cultural data.

climax.text: No verified backup existed. Twelve years of music history vanished in one botched migration.

punchline.text: Verify backups before you migrate. Test the restore path.

quiz.question: What happened to MySpace's music during the server migration?
options: 50 million songs were lost because backups were not verified
The music was moved to a new platform successfully
Users chose to delete their own music
